description: Sector overlay for banks, payment institutions, fintech platforms, lending products, wallets, financial services providers, and related financial-sector documentation in South Africa.

metadata.scope: South Africa-only sector overlay controls for Banking and Fintech. Intended to be used with south_africa_core_control_library.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from official South African legal/regulatory sources available at rule-pack publication time. This starter pack is not legal advice and requires expert validation before production reliance.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.

primary_sources.name: Financial Sector Regulation Act 9 of 2017
primary_sources.issuer: Republic of South Africa / National Treasury / SARB / FSCA

primary_sources.name: Banks Act 94 of 1990
primary_sources.issuer: Republic of South Africa / Prudential Authority

primary_sources.name: Financial Intelligence Centre Act, 2001 and FIC compliance guidance
Protection of Personal Information Act, 2013 (POPIA)
primary_sources.issuer: Information Regulator / Republic of South Africa

primary_sources.name: Consumer Protection Act, 2008
primary_sources.issuer: Republic of South Africa / National Consumer Commission

rules.title: Financial-sector licence, registration, or regulated-status evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for bank, fintech, payment-service, FSP, Prudential Authority, FSCA, SARB, PASA, or regulated status evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: CDD, KYC, RMCP, and sanctions-screening evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for customer due diligence, KYC, beneficial ownership, RMCP, sanctions screening, PEP screening, or suspicious-transaction reporting evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Payment-system, settlement, or payment-service operating evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for payment-system participation, clearing, settlement, payment instrument, scheme rules, or transaction monitoring evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Financial data privacy and security safeguards are documented
rules.summary: Checks for POPIA, personal information, account data, security safeguards, breach notification, or data-subject rights evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Financial customer disclosure, complaint, and fair-treatment evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for product disclosure, fees, complaints, redress, fair treatment, or customer communication evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.
